message:
fix(fcp-store): unbreak scrub_corrupt_symbols_locked calls after per-object RwLock refactor

REVIEW R2 fresh-eyes pass (cc_1) found that commit 6ad692b4
(br-tlspb — per-object lock refactor of MemorySymbolStore) broke
the crate's lib build. Two call sites pass `obj` — now a
`RwLockWriteGuard<'_, ObjectSymbols>` under the new nested-lock
layout — to `scrub_corrupt_symbols_locked(obj: &mut ObjectSymbols)`:

  symbol_store.rs:304  Self::scrub_corrupt_symbols_locked(obj)
  symbol_store.rs:502  Self::scrub_corrupt_symbols_locked(obj)

Rust does not auto-ref/deref arguments at free / associated function
call sites, so both trip E0308:

    error[E0308]: mismatched types
       --> crates/fcp-store/src/symbol_store.rs:304:48
        |
    304 |       Self::scrub_corrupt_symbols_locked(obj)
        |                                          ^^^ expected `&mut ObjectSymbols`,
        |                                              found `RwLockWriteGuard<'_, ObjectSymbols>`

The crate did not compile on main (`cargo check -p fcp-store --lib`
failed), which broke every downstream consumer (fcp-mesh, fwc, fcp-
host, ...).

Fix: pass `&mut obj` at both call sites, which deref-coerces the
RwLockWriteGuard to `&mut ObjectSymbols`. Zero runtime change.
Added a short comment on the scrub_one_object call explaining the
coercion so the pattern survives the next refactor.

Verified: cargo check -p fcp-store --lib clean; cargo test -p
fcp-store --lib 808 pass (same 2 pre-existing flaky quarantine::
evict_expired* failures; baseline-equivalent to ywpup / fm746 /
s5u65 / aof5n sessions).

This commit is the genuine close-out for br-tlspb's per-object-lock
rollout — a91a3ac0 only fixed a test helper and didn't catch the
compile error.

Cc_1 / SunnyMoose REVIEW-MODE R2 P2
